propositions: Register HmacScheme as a first-class HMAC-SHA256 registry scheme implementing the registry Scheme protocol.

HmacScheme.verify computes HMAC-SHA256 over DSSE-PAE bytes using trust_root key and constant-time-compares to the first signature, returning certified/mismatch/foreign_key verdicts and failing closed on empty key or missing signatures.
register_legacy_schemes() is idempotent and pins POLICY entries for plan-review and completion-verifier to scheme HMAC-SHA256 in namespace rebar-attest-hmac, called once at package import.
Existing legacy attestation records (canonical hex-HMAC signatures) are NOT rerouted through the registry and keep verifying unchanged via the untouched signing.py path.
This is the expand phase only; the HMAC-to-asymmetric cutover and re-enveloping of old records belong to the out-of-scope op-cert epic contract phase.
Regression guard: signing.py sign/verify functions remain unmodified and a plan-review manifest still verifies as certified.
